title: thymus serpyllum f. parvifolius
intro: Thymus serpyllum f. parvifolius, also known as Dwarf Thyme, is a subspecies of the Thymus serpyllum plant, a low-growing, flowering herb native to Europe and Western Asia. It possesses antiseptic, antispasmodic, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Dwarf Thyme include its ability to relieve coughs, soothe digestive issues, and reduce stress, offering numerous health benefits such as improved respiratory function, enhanced immune system, and better sleep quality. Dwarf Thyme can be prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used in herbal medicine, as well as being a popular ingredient in cooking and aromatherapy.
